text:
Sunday, September 25, 2016: Toronto
15:00-16:30
CN Tower EdgeWalk (Optional)
Location: CN Tower (301 Front Street West)
Details: Enjoy the view of the CN Tower from the outside by walking hands-free around 
the circumference of the roof of the CN Tower. A special Walk suit will be provided that 
is worn over top of your clothing, hats, glove and jackets will be provided according to 
weather conditions. Please dress appropriately for the weather conditions. For more 
information: http://www.edgewalkcntower.ca/faq.html
18:15
Depart from Fairmont Royal York Hotel (100 Front Street West)
18:30
Arrive at Mercatto Restaurant (Eaton Centre, 220 Yonge Street)
18:30-20:00
6th ACE Welcome Reception
Location: Mercatto Restaurant 
Welcome: Mitch Davies (Assistant Deputy Minister, Strategic Policy Sector of 
Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada)
Remarks: Suneet Singh Tuli (Founder and CEO, DataWind Ltd), Ambassador Neil Parsan 
(Executive Secretary for Integral Development, Organization of American States)
Keynote: Mamdouh Shoukri (President, York University)
20:00
Depart from Mercatto Restaurant (Eaton Centre, 220 Yonge Street)
20:15
Arrive at Fairmont Royal York Hotel (100 Front Street West)
After 20:15
Personal Time
Monday, September 26, 2016: Toronto
7:30
Meet in lobby of Fairmont Royal York Hotel and board bus
7:40
Depart from Fairmont Royal York Hotel (100 Front Street West)
ITINERARY
Please mind the version date on the itinerary as further refinements can be expected.
Page 1 of 14
23/09/2016

8:05-8:30
Arrive and disembark bus at MaRS Discovery District (101 College Street), then walk to 
MaRS Atrium
8:30-9:25
Breakfast and Opening Ceremony
Location: Atrium, MaRS Discovery District
Welcome: Ilse Treurnicht (CEO, MaRS Discovery District)
Protocol welcome from indigenous community
Keynote: The Honourable Navdeep Bains (Minister of Innovation, Science and 
Economic Development Canada)
Thought Leader: Meric Gertler (President, University of Toronto) on importance of 
innovation and place
9:25-10:00
Innovation Policy Panel Discussion
Details: Focus on federal Innovation Agenda and Ontario's innovation policies
Moderator: Ilse Treurnicht (CEO, MaRS Discovery District)
Panelists: John Knubley (Deputy Minister, Innovation Science and Economic 
Development Canada), Giles Gherson (Deputy Minister, Ontario Ministry of Research 
and Innovation, Ontario Ministry of Economic Development, Employment and 
Infrastructure), Jay Williams (U.S. Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Economic 
Development)
10:00
Walk to and board bus at MaRS Discovery District
10:15
Depart from MaRS Discovery District (101 College Street)
Details: Southern Ontario Water Consortium to provide an overview of Ontario's water 
innovation ecosystem, policy and support for technology development
Speakers on bus: Brenda Lucas (Executive Director, Southern Ontario Water 
Consortium)
11:00
Arrive and disembark bus at Lakeview Water Treatment Plant (920 East Avenue, 
Mississauga)

Tour of Lakeview Water Treatment Plant
Location: 920 East Avenue, Mississauga
Details: Delegation will be divided into four groups to tour state-of-the-art, award 
winning facility.
12:00-12:15
Walk to boardroom and serve lunch
12:15-13:00
Working Lunch: Panel of Presentations from water technology companies
Location: Boardroom
Details: Presentations over lunch on low cost solutions to infrastructure monitoring and

Moderator: Josh Chong (WaterTAP) 
Speakers: Maryam Eyvazy (Inside Sales Associate, UV Pure Technologies Inc.), Fabian 
Papa (Consulting Engineer, Hydratek), Mike Stadnyckyj (Director of Marketing and 
Communications, Echologics), Ted Mao (VP of Research, Trojan Technologies)
13:00-13:10
Wellness break
Location: Boardroom

23/09/2016

Board bus at Lakeview Water Treatment Plant

Details: Video about Toronto to be played on bus

Life Sciences Program: Welcome and Scene-Setting Remarks

University of Toronto and Affiliated Hospitals: Research Collaboration Models

Details: Focus on research collaboration in the fields of regenerative medicine,

Wellness Break

Panel on Commercialization of Science Innovations
Location: JLABS @ Toronto, MaRS Discovery District
Details: Focus on medial research commercialization and role of incubators; and

Moderator: Rafi Hofstein (President and CEO, MaRS Innovation)

Moody (VP of Commercialization, Centre for Commercialization of Regenerative 
Medicine), Linda Maxwell (Director, Ryerson’s Biomedical Zone), Rebecca Yu (Head of

Details: Opportunity to speak with panelists and presenters
17:30
Walk to and board bus at MaRS Discovery District
17:45
Depart from MaRS Discovery District (101 College Street)
18:00
Arrive and disembark bus at Aria Ristorante (25 York Street)

Meet in lobby of Fairmont Royal York Hotel and board bus

Lunch Panel on Fintech

Details: Presentation on the history of the Kitchener-Waterloo region and how it grew

Breakfast
Location: 1st floor, Sedra Student Design Centre, E5, University of Waterloo
Details: The Sedra Student Design Centre is the largest facility of its kind in North

workbays, sand and paint shops, an electromagnetic radiation lab, and engine test labs.

Location: 1st floor, Sedra Student Design Centre, E5, University of Waterloo

Speakers: The Honourable Bardish Chagger (Minister of Small Business and Tourism

(President and Vice-Chancellor, University of Waterloo)

Kitchener-Waterloo

Details: Bus presentation on how Canada's historic steel making centre is building on its

Advanced Manufacturing Tours
Location: McMaster Automotive Resource Centre (MARC) and CanmetMATERIALS (183 
Longwood Road South)
Details: Delegation will be divided into groups. Wellness break incorporated in tours. 
MARC is one of Canada’s leading research facilities in electric and hybrid vehicles where 
researchers, students and industry professionals are working to resolve the issues facing 
the automotive industry. CanmetMATERIALS is the largest research centre in Canada 
dedicated fabricating, processing and evaluating metals and materials.
Welcome: Nick Markettos (Assistant VP, Research Partnerships, McMaster University) 
and Philippe Dauphin (Director General, CanmetMATERIALS)
Tour of Centre for Mechatronics & Hybrid Technology (MARC): Cam Fisher (Industry 
Liaison, Centre for Mechatronics & Hybrid Technology), Saeid Habibi (Director, Centre

Engineering), Joey Kish (Associate Professor, Materials Science & Engineering)

Director of the McMaster Centre for Software Certification), Alan Wassyng (Director of 
the McMaster Centre for Software Certification)
Tour of CanmetMATERIALS: Philippe Dauphin (Director General, CanmetMATERIALS)
Hamilton

Location: Southbrook Vineyards
Speaker: Bill Redelmeier (Owner, Southbrook Vineyards)
Details: Unique winery in that it is completely organic and biodynamic. Southbrook 
Vineyards uses less water, less energy, no synthetic pesticides, no chemical fertilizer, no 
bioengineering, and no genetic modification. Delegation will be divided into two groups.
